destinations.evidence: The fee functions are pure arithmetic over their explicit inputs for long-running HTTP polling and agent work metrics.
Verifier settlement validation recomputes fees through the same deterministic fee functions used by builder-side settlement creation.
destinations.snippet:
let fees = calculate_async_fees(
    &mut db,
    get_model_registry_address(),
    job.precompile_address,
    &precompile_input,
    Some(&spc_output),
    &mut model_cache,
)?;
...
calculate_agent_fees_phase2_work(iterations, tool_calls)
...
calculate_async_fees_phase2(metadata.address, blocks_polled, delivery_result.as_ref().map_or(0, |result| result.len()))

equivalence_argument: The fee paths do not depend on wall clock, random state, or mutable global state for the listed components; identical inputs lead builder and verifier through the same arithmetic functions.
notes: LLM short-settlement pricing still depends on explicit model registry state passed through ModelCache, while this invariant covers HTTP, long-running HTTP Phase 2, and agent Phase 2 fee determinism.
rationale: The added differential tests explicitly frame fee calculation as consensus-critical builder/verifier equivalence and assert repeated calls with the same inputs return equal fee values. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: HTTP executor fees, long-running HTTP Phase 2 fees, and Agent Phase 2 fees must be deterministic for identical inputs so builder and verifier compute the same fee components.
suggested_falsification_tests: For a fixed set of HTTP input/output lengths, Agent iterations/tool_calls, and Phase 2 commit/settlement/delivery lengths, compute fees through the builder and verifier paths and assert all executor, commitment, and inclusion fee components are identical.
summary: Async fee calculations are deterministic for matching inputs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For a fixed set of HTTP input/output lengths, Agent iterations/tool_calls, and Phase 2 commit/settlement/delivery lengths, compute fees through the builder and verifier paths and assert all executor, commitment, and inclusion fee components are identical.
title: Async fee calculations are deterministic
